behavior: Soars and glides over open ground, often hunting from a perch or by hovering into the wind before a controlled dive.
field_marks: Large, bulky buteo with very broad, rounded wings and spread “fingered” wingtips.
Usually rich brown above and paler below, often with a darker streaked belly band.
Adult tail is typically cinnamon-red above and pale below; juveniles have a brown, narrowly banded tail.
Underwings commonly show pale flight feathers contrasted with dark leading-edge bars and darker wingtips.
Short, strongly hooked dark bill; eyes and bare legs/feet appear yellow to pale yellow.
Plumage is variable: light, rufous, and dark morphs occur.
habitat: Open country and habitat edges: fields, grasslands, meadows, shrublands, woodland margins, and other open areas with scattered perches or trees.

sources.title: Red-tailed Hawk Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
